title: feat(stores): add MySQL storage adapter
summary: Adds a new @mastra/mysql package implementing the full MastraStorage interface for MySQL. Covers all 14 domain stores (agents, memory, workflows, datasets, experiments, observability, blobs, scores, skills, MCP clients/servers, prompt blocks, scorer definitions, workspaces). Uses mysql2/promise with connection pooling. Includes 372 integration/unit tests, a Docker Compose for local MySQL 8.0, changeset, tsup build config, and follows the stores/mssql pattern. Also adds MySQLStore to the sort whitelist in stores/_test-utils. CodeRabbit flagged several actionable items: non-transactional multi-step mutations risking partial writes, use of 'any' types where stricter typing is preferred, and potential race conditions in version number computation.
maintainerNotes: Large but well-structured community contribution adding MySQL as a storage backend. The PR follows existing monorepo conventions (mirroring stores/mssql). Author claims 372 tests passing locally but CI checks remain in PENDING state (likely need secrets/environment setup for MySQL). CodeRabbit raised substantive concerns: (1) several multi-step create/delete operations are not wrapped in transactions, risking partial writes on failure; (2) pervasive use of 'any' types in safeParseJSON and queryParams; (3) version number computation has a TOCTOU race. No human reviews yet—only CodeRabbit automated reviews. Vercel deploy checks fail due to authorization (expected for external contributors). The single changed file outside the new package is a 1-line addition to stores/_test-utils sort whitelist. Needs a maintainer to review the transactional safety concerns, validate test coverage runs in CI, and confirm the package meets quality standards before merge.
